originalBehavior: The published blockchain page exposes five editable blocks where changing a block field recalculates that block hash, propagates previous-hash updates downstream, and marks invalid blocks visually until they are re-mined.
localStatus: The local root keeps the published chain editor, hash recalculation, mining controls, and downstream invalidation behavior intact under the top-level blockchain slug.
notes: The local root intentionally maps to the upstream blockchain scene rather than the video landing page.
Editable block number, nonce, data, previous-hash, hash, and mine-button fields still use the published jQuery handlers.
evidence: Implementation planning treated live hash updates, nonce mining, and visible validity changes as parity-critical behavior.
Shared smoke coverage edits block data, verifies the root hash and downstream previous-hash values change, then mines the first block and checks the validity classes update.

originalBehavior: The published demo also includes earlier warmup pages for a standalone SHA256 hash and a single editable block before the full chain view.
localStatus: The local route keeps those supporting pages as sibling files under the same route tree so the original demo progression remains available locally.
notes: These pages keep the published field IDs and inline handlers instead of being rewritten as new components.
The hash and block scenes share the same vendored legacy runtime as the main blockchain page.
evidence: The local navigation still exposes Hash and Block alongside the main Blockchain page.
Route coverage verifies the supporting local pages load under the nested static mount.

originalBehavior: The published family extends the basic chain into distributed, token, and coinbase views with multiple peer chains and richer transaction fields.
localStatus: The local route keeps those additional scenes under the blockchain asset tree so peer-comparison and transaction-heavy pages remain available locally.
notes: The distributed view still mounts multiple peer chains from the original markup.
Token and coinbase pages continue to rely on the published block and transaction serialization logic.
evidence: Implementation planning treated any distributed-ledger or multi-chain comparison view as parity-critical when the live demo shipped it.
Shared smoke coverage loads the distributed page locally and verifies the peer-chain mount points render without broken asset paths.

originalBehavior: The published family used root-relative blockchain asset paths and also shipped a separate video landing page with author and repo callouts outside the interactive scenes.
localStatus: The local copy rewrites page-local asset paths, canonical metadata, and navbar links for the subpath-hosted replica while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface.
notes: The public entry route no longer surfaces the upstream landing page, GitHub ribbon, or donation copy.
Each vendored HTML page now loads its own local CSS and JS tree instead of root-relative Anders paths.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks hardcoded upstream promo or share surfaces outside approved footer configuration.
Implementation planning called for patching only shell and URL behavior while keeping the legacy runtime intact.
